- RCE Fabric Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 WEN:
- Write firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 REN:
- Read firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 5:0 0x0 OWNER:

the ARF registers.

- Size of the Address Range Firewall in bytes. Reset value of 0x00000
indicates firewall is disengaged. Bits [1:0] are tied off in design to 00 and writes to these bits are dropped.
- SW can assume these bits to be implemented and directly configure the
size in bytes Size[31:0] = 0x0 : Reset value. Illegal if ARF.WEN/ARF.REN = 1.
- Size[31:0] = 0x4 : bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x8 : 8 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x10 : 16 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x20 : 32 bytes protected
...
- Size[31:0] = 0x8000000 :128MB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x40000000 : 1GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x80000000 : 2GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0xFFFFFFFC : 4GB-4Bytes protected
- RCE_FIREWALL_ARF_18_BASE_ADDR_LOW_0

31:2 0x0 ADDR:
- Lower 32 bits of the physical address indicating the start of the
- Address Range Firewall
- Bits [1:0] forced to 00 to align to 4B address

- RCE_FIREWALL_ARF_19_CTL_0
- Offset: 0x30270
- Read/Write: R/W
- Parity Protection: N
- Shadow: N
- SCR Protection: 0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b00xx,xxxx,xxxx,xx00,xxxx,xxxx,xx00,0000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 ARF_LCK: Overall ARF Lock Bit for writing into ARF registers. Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset. Reads of ARF registers is allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to any ARF registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to all ARF registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE

- RCE Fabric Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 ADR_LCK:
- Address and Size register lock bit
Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset.
- Reads of BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers is
allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 WEN:
- Write firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 REN:
- Read firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 5:0 0x0 OWNER:
- Master with MSTRID=OWNER can update the ARF registers, as long as the
ARF_LCK bit is not set. No other Master is allowed to update the settings.
- Reset value of 0x0 allows Masters which can drive AWPROT[1]=0 to update
the ARF registers.

0x0 ARF_LCK: Overall ARF Lock Bit for writing into ARF registers. Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset. Reads of ARF registers is allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to any ARF registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to all ARF registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 ADR_LCK:
- Address and Size register lock bit
Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset.
- Reads of BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers is
allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE

- RCE Fabric Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 WEN:
- Write firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 REN:
- Read firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 5:0 0x0 OWNER:
- Master with MSTRID=OWNER can update the ARF registers, as long as the
ARF_LCK bit is not set. No other Master is allowed to update the settings.
- Reset value of 0x0 allows Masters which can drive AWPROT[1]=0 to update
the ARF registers.

- RCE_FIREWALL_ARF_21_CTL_0

- RCE Fabric Registers
- Offset: 0x302b0
- Read/Write: R/W
- Parity Protection: N
- Shadow: N
- SCR Protection: 0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b00xx,xxxx,xxxx,xx00,xxxx,xxxx,xx00,0000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 ARF_LCK: Overall ARF Lock Bit for writing into ARF registers. Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset. Reads of ARF registers is allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to any ARF registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to all ARF registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 ADR_LCK:
- Address and Size register lock bit
Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset.
- Reads of BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers is
allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 WEN:
- Write firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 REN:
- Read firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 5:0 0x0 OWNER:
- Master with MSTRID=OWNER can update the ARF registers, as long as the
ARF_LCK bit is not set. No other Master is allowed to update the settings.
- Reset value of 0x0 allows Masters which can drive AWPROT[1]=0 to update
the ARF registers.
- RCE_FIREWALL_ARF_21_SIZE_0
- Offset: 0x302b4
- Read/Write: R/W

- RCE Fabric Registers
- Parity Protection: N
- Shadow: N
- SCR Protection: 0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,00xx)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
31:2 0x0 SIZE:
- Size of the Address Range Firewall in bytes. Reset value of 0x00000
indicates firewall is disengaged. Bits [1:0] are tied off in design to 00 and writes to these bits are dropped.
- SW can assume these bits to be implemented and directly configure the
size in bytes Size[31:0] = 0x0 : Reset value. Illegal if ARF.WEN/ARF.REN = 1.
- Size[31:0] = 0x4 : bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x8 : 8 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x10 : 16 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x20 : 32 bytes protected
...
- Size[31:0] = 0x8000000 :128MB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x40000000 : 1GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x80000000 : 2GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0xFFFFFFFC : 4GB-4Bytes protected
- RCE_FIREWALL_ARF_21_BASE_ADDR_LOW_0

- Read/Write: R/W
- Parity Protection: N
- Shadow: N
- SCR Protection: 0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,00xx)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
31:2 0x0 ADDR:
- Lower 32 bits of the physical address indicating the start of the
- Address Range Firewall
- Bits [1:0] forced to 00 to align to 4B address

- Shadow: N

- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 ARF_LCK: Overall ARF Lock Bit for writing into ARF registers. Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset. Reads of ARF registers is allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to any ARF registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to all ARF registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE

- RCE Fabric Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 ADR_LCK:
- Address and Size register lock bit
Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset.
- Reads of BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers is
allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 WEN:
- Write firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 REN:
- Read firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 5:0 0x0 OWNER:
- Master with MSTRID=OWNER can update the ARF registers, as long as the
ARF_LCK bit is not set. No other Master is allowed to update the settings.
- Reset value of 0x0 allows Masters which can drive AWPROT[1]=0 to update
the ARF registers.

- Bit
- Reset
- Description
31:2 0x0 SIZE:
- Size of the Address Range Firewall in bytes. Reset value of 0x00000
indicates firewall is disengaged. Bits [1:0] are tied off in design to 00 and writes to these bits are dropped.
- SW can assume these bits to be implemented and directly configure the
size in bytes Size[31:0] = 0x0 : Reset value. Illegal if ARF.WEN/ARF.REN = 1.
- Size[31:0] = 0x4 : bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x8 : 8 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x10 : 16 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x20 : 32 bytes protected
...
- Size[31:0] = 0x8000000 :128MB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x40000000 : 1GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x80000000 : 2GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0xFFFFFFFC : 4GB-4Bytes protected

0x0 MSTRID_3: 1 = Allow write access for MSTRID=3 0 = Disallow write access for MSTRID=3 0x0 MSTRID_2: 1 = Allow write access for MSTRID=2 0 = Disallow write access for MSTRID=2 0x0 MSTRID_1: 1 = Allow write access for MSTRID=1 0 = Disallow write access for MSTRID=1 0x0 TZ: 1 = Allow write access for Masters with AWPPROT[1]=0 0 = Disallow write access for Masters with AWPROT[1]=0

- Parity Protection: N
- Shadow: N
- SCR Protection: 0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b00xx,xxxx,xxxx,xx00,xxxx,xxxx,xx00,0000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 ARF_LCK: Overall ARF Lock Bit for writing into ARF registers. Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset. Reads of ARF registers is allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to any ARF registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to all ARF registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 ADR_LCK:
- Address and Size register lock bit
Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset.
- Reads of BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers is
allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE

- RCE Fabric Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 WEN:
- Write firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 REN:
- Read firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 5:0 0x0 OWNER:
- Master with MSTRID=OWNER can update the ARF registers, as long as the
ARF_LCK bit is not set. No other Master is allowed to update the settings.
- Reset value of 0x0 allows Masters which can drive AWPROT[1]=0 to update
the ARF registers.
- RCE_FIREWALL_ARF_23_SIZE_0

- Shadow: N
- SCR Protection: 0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,00xx)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
31:2 0x0 SIZE:
- Size of the Address Range Firewall in bytes. Reset value of 0x00000
indicates firewall is disengaged. Bits [1:0] are tied off in design to 00 and writes to these bits are dropped.
- SW can assume these bits to be implemented and directly configure the
size in bytes Size[31:0] = 0x0 : Reset value. Illegal if ARF.WEN/ARF.REN = 1.
- Size[31:0] = 0x4 : bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x8 : 8 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x10 : 16 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x20 : 32 bytes protected
...
- Size[31:0] = 0x8000000 :128MB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x40000000 : 1GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x80000000 : 2GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0xFFFFFFFC : 4GB-4Bytes protected

- Description
0x0 ARF_LCK: Overall ARF Lock Bit for writing into ARF registers. Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset. Reads of ARF registers is allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to any ARF registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to all ARF registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 ADR_LCK:
- Address and Size register lock bit
Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset.
- Reads of BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers is
allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 WEN:
- Write firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 REN:
- Read firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 5:0 0x0 OWNER:
- Master with MSTRID=OWNER can update the ARF registers, as long as the
ARF_LCK bit is not set. No other Master is allowed to update the settings.
- Reset value of 0x0 allows Masters which can drive AWPROT[1]=0 to update
the ARF registers.

- RCE_FIREWALL_ARF_26_CTL_0
- Offset: 0x30350
- Read/Write: R/W
- Parity Protection: N
- Shadow: N
- SCR Protection: 0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b00xx,xxxx,xxxx,xx00,xxxx,xxxx,xx00,0000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 ARF_LCK: Overall ARF Lock Bit for writing into ARF registers. Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset. Reads of ARF registers is allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to any ARF registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to all ARF registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 ADR_LCK:
- Address and Size register lock bit
Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset.
- Reads of BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers is
allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE

- RCE Fabric Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 WEN:
- Write firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 REN:
- Read firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 5:0 0x0 OWNER:
- Master with MSTRID=OWNER can update the ARF registers, as long as the
ARF_LCK bit is not set. No other Master is allowed to update the settings.
- Reset value of 0x0 allows Masters which can drive AWPROT[1]=0 to update
the ARF registers.
- RCE_FIREWALL_ARF_26_SIZE_0
- Offset: 0x30354
- Read/Write: R/W
- Parity Protection: N
- Shadow: N
- SCR Protection: 0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,00xx)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
31:2 0x0 SIZE:
- Size of the Address Range Firewall in bytes. Reset value of 0x00000
indicates firewall is disengaged. Bits [1:0] are tied off in design to 00 and writes to these bits are dropped.
- SW can assume these bits to be implemented and directly configure the
size in bytes Size[31:0] = 0x0 : Reset value. Illegal if ARF.WEN/ARF.REN = 1.
- Size[31:0] = 0x4 : bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x8 : 8 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x10 : 16 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x20 : 32 bytes protected
...
- Size[31:0] = 0x8000000 :128MB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x40000000 : 1GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x80000000 : 2GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0xFFFFFFFC : 4GB-4Bytes protected
- RCE_FIREWALL_ARF_26_BASE_ADDR_LOW_0

- Reset
- Description
31:2 0x0 ADDR:
- Lower 32 bits of the physical address indicating the start of the
- Address Range Firewall
- Bits [1:0] forced to 00 to align to 4B address

- Description
0x0 ARF_LCK: Overall ARF Lock Bit for writing into ARF registers. Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset. Reads of ARF registers is allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to any ARF registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to all ARF registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 ADR_LCK:
- Address and Size register lock bit
Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset.
- Reads of BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers is
allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 WEN:
- Write firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 REN:
- Read firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 5:0 0x0 OWNER:
- Master with MSTRID=OWNER can update the ARF registers, as long as the
ARF_LCK bit is not set. No other Master is allowed to update the settings.
- Reset value of 0x0 allows Masters which can drive AWPROT[1]=0 to update
the ARF registers.

- RCE_FIREWALL_ARF_28_CTL_0
- Offset: 0x30390
- Read/Write: R/W
- Parity Protection: N
- Shadow: N
- SCR Protection: 0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b00xx,xxxx,xxxx,xx00,xxxx,xxxx,xx00,0000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 ARF_LCK: Overall ARF Lock Bit for writing into ARF registers. Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset. Reads of ARF registers is allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to any ARF registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to all ARF registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE

- RCE Fabric Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 ADR_LCK:
- Address and Size register lock bit
Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset.
- Reads of BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers is
allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 WEN:
- Write firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 REN:
- Read firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 5:0 0x0 OWNER:
- Master with MSTRID=OWNER can update the ARF registers, as long as the
ARF_LCK bit is not set. No other Master is allowed to update the settings.
- Reset value of 0x0 allows Masters which can drive AWPROT[1]=0 to update
the ARF registers.
- RCE_FIREWALL_ARF_28_SIZE_0
- Offset: 0x30394
- Read/Write: R/W
- Parity Protection: N
- Shadow: N
- SCR Protection: 0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,00xx)

- RCE Fabric Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
31:2 0x0 SIZE:
- Size of the Address Range Firewall in bytes. Reset value of 0x00000
indicates firewall is disengaged. Bits [1:0] are tied off in design to 00 and writes to these bits are dropped.
- SW can assume these bits to be implemented and directly configure the
size in bytes Size[31:0] = 0x0 : Reset value. Illegal if ARF.WEN/ARF.REN = 1.
- Size[31:0] = 0x4 : bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x8 : 8 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x10 : 16 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x20 : 32 bytes protected
...
- Size[31:0] = 0x8000000 :128MB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x40000000 : 1GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x80000000 : 2GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0xFFFFFFFC : 4GB-4Bytes protected

- Bit

- Description
0x0 ARF_LCK: Overall ARF Lock Bit for writing into ARF registers. Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset. Reads of ARF registers is allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to any ARF registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to all ARF registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 ADR_LCK:
- Address and Size register lock bit
Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset.
- Reads of BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers is
allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE

- RCE Fabric Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 WEN:
- Write firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 REN:
- Read firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 5:0 0x0 OWNER:
- Master with MSTRID=OWNER can update the ARF registers, as long as the
ARF_LCK bit is not set. No other Master is allowed to update the settings.
- Reset value of 0x0 allows Masters which can drive AWPROT[1]=0 to update
the ARF registers.

- Description
31:2 0x0 SIZE:
- Size of the Address Range Firewall in bytes. Reset value of 0x00000
indicates firewall is disengaged. Bits [1:0] are tied off in design to 00 and writes to these bits are dropped.
- SW can assume these bits to be implemented and directly configure the
size in bytes Size[31:0] = 0x0 : Reset value. Illegal if ARF.WEN/ARF.REN = 1.
- Size[31:0] = 0x4 : bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x8 : 8 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x10 : 16 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x20 : 32 bytes protected
...
- Size[31:0] = 0x8000000 :128MB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x40000000 : 1GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x80000000 : 2GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0xFFFFFFFC : 4GB-4Bytes protected
- RCE_FIREWALL_ARF_30_BASE_ADDR_LOW_0

- Read/Write: R/W
- Parity Protection: N
- Shadow: N
- SCR Protection: 0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,00xx)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
31:2 0x0 ADDR:
- Lower 32 bits of the physical address indicating the start of the
- Address Range Firewall
- Bits [1:0] forced to 00 to align to 4B address

- RCE_FIREWALL_ARF_31_CTL_0
- Offset: 0x303f0
- Read/Write: R/W
- Parity Protection: N
- Shadow: N
- SCR Protection: 0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b00xx,xxxx,xxxx,xx00,xxxx,xxxx,xx00,0000)
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 ARF_LCK: Overall ARF Lock Bit for writing into ARF registers. Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset. Reads of ARF registers is allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to any ARF registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to all ARF registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE

- RCE Fabric Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
0x0 ADR_LCK:
- Address and Size register lock bit
Sticky, once set cannot be cleared until next secure reset.
- Reads of BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers is
allowed irrespective of the setting of this bit. 1 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers NOT allowed and return with slverr. 0 = Write accesses to ARF BASE_ADDRESS_HIGH, BASE_ADDRESS_LOW, SIZE registers allowed to MSTRID=OWNER field in this register. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 WEN:
- Write firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for write accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 0x0 REN:
- Read firewall check enable
1 = Enable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = Disable firewall checks for read accesses through the firewall. 0 = DISABLE 1 = ENABLE 5:0 0x0 OWNER:
- Master with MSTRID=OWNER can update the ARF registers, as long as the
ARF_LCK bit is not set. No other Master is allowed to update the settings.
- Reset value of 0x0 allows Masters which can drive AWPROT[1]=0 to update
the ARF registers.
- RCE_FIREWALL_ARF_31_SIZE_0
- Offset: 0x303f4
- Read/Write: R/W
- Parity Protection: N
- Shadow: N
- SCR Protection: 0
- Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,00xx)

- RCE Fabric Registers
- Bit
- Reset
- Description
31:2 0x0 SIZE:
- Size of the Address Range Firewall in bytes. Reset value of 0x00000
indicates firewall is disengaged. Bits [1:0] are tied off in design to 00 and writes to these bits are dropped.
- SW can assume these bits to be implemented and directly configure the
size in bytes Size[31:0] = 0x0 : Reset value. Illegal if ARF.WEN/ARF.REN = 1.
- Size[31:0] = 0x4 : bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x8 : 8 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x10 : 16 bytes protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x20 : 32 bytes protected
...
- Size[31:0] = 0x8000000 :128MB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x40000000 : 1GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0x80000000 : 2GB protected
- Size[31:0] = 0xFFFFFFFC : 4GB-4Bytes protected

##### 7.2.3.20.3 RCE Address Space Translator Registers
There are two instances of the RCE Address Space Translator (AST) Registers, by the name of RCE_AST_0 and RCE_AST_1. For details of the registers descriptions and base addresses, refer to the Address Space Translator (AST) Registers in the Address Space Translation (AST) chapter.

- Image Signal Processor (ISP)}.Body)
